operating_state: Net sales increased 22% to $14.1 billion in Q3 2022 driven by production and precision agriculture segment growth, small agriculture and turf expansion, and construction and forestry increases. Nine-month revenue reached $37.0 billion, up 13% from prior year. Supply chain pressures and production inefficiencies persisted but underlying demand fundamentals remained strong across all segments.
cost_structure: Cost of sales increased with cost-to-sales ratio rising to 73.2% in Q3 from 72.7% due to higher material costs and production volumes partially offset by price realization. Research and development expenses rose reflecting continued investment in precision agriculture and automation technologies. Selling, administrative and general expenses increased driven by merit pay increases and business expansion activities.
material_events: Acquired majority ownership in Kreisel Electric and completed acquisition of three former Deere-Hitachi joint venture excavator factories, generating a non-cash gain on previously held equity investment. Suspended shipments to Russia resulting in pretax expense for asset impairments and reserves. Issued sustainability-linked medium-term notes with potential ESG performance-linked rate increases and ratified UAW collective bargaining agreement with lump sum payment expensed.
macro_exposures: Russia/Ukraine events resulted in suspended shipments, asset impairments, and Russian government restrictions on cash repatriation leading to reclassification of excess cash as restricted assets. Foreign exchange translation adjustments reduced comprehensive income during the nine-month period. The Inflation Reduction Act introduced corporate minimum tax though initial analysis suggests no material impact on financial statements.
forward_outlook: Management expects favorable conditions to continue into fiscal 2023 based on strong underlying fundamentals and customer responses to early-order programs, with factories and suppliers preparing for higher demand levels. The company anticipates generating excess operating cash flows in 2022 and expects long-term cash flow generation supported by strong operating segment fundamentals.
uncertainty_profile: Unresolved legal actions across product liability, employment, patent, and antitrust matters are not expected to materially affect consolidated financial statements. Supply chain disruptions, commodity price volatility, and cybersecurity risks across information technology infrastructure continue presenting operational uncertainties. Unsatisfied performance obligations for contracts exceeding one year extend through 2027 and beyond.
